Title: Two Aquaporin Genes, GhPIP2;7 and GhTIP2;1, Positively Regulate the Tolerance of Upland Cotton to Salt and Osmotic Stresses

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Silencing of the closely related PIP genes GhPIP2;2 and GhPIP2;3 did not significantly affect salt tolerance, suggesting GhPIP2;7 has a more specific, non-redundant role in salt stress response.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq time-course of G. hirsutum TM-1 leaves under 200 mM NaCl used to assess GhAQP expression patterns; supplemented with qRT-PCR under 150 mM NaCl.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhPIP2;7 in cotton markedly decreases salt tolerance, causing stronger leaf wilting and browning, reduced plant height, lower chlorophyll content, and impaired antioxidant enzyme activities under high salinity.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Virus-induced silencing of GhPIP2;7 reduces SOD and POD activities and increases MDA accumulation under salt stress, indicating compromised ROS-scavenging capacity and aggravated membrane lipid peroxidation.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Positive regulator in the salt stress response pathway, likely contributing to maintenance of water transport and antioxidant defense under salinity.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aquaporin, plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP) subfamily
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GhPIP2;7 is significantly induced by 150 mM NaCl and shows peak expression at 3 h after salt treatment, then decreases towards 48 h.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress treatments: primarily 150 mM NaCl for expression profiling and 400 mM NaCl irrigation or leaf-disk incubation for functional assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (salt stress) in upland cotton
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 400 mM NaCl, GhPIP2;7-silenced plants show significantly reduced SOD and POD activities and significantly increased MDA content relative to mock controls; chlorophyll content in leaf disks is significantly decreased (exact values shown in Figure 4).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: GhPIP2;7-silenced plants display yellowing, wilting, dwarfing, and more severe browning of leaf disks than mock after salt treatment; no obvious phenotype under normal conditions.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: G. hirsutum cultivars GX100-2 and Zhong79 for qRT-PCR and stress assays; GX100-2 used for VIGS with CLCrV-based vectors targeting GhPIP2;7; mock plants transformed with empty CLCrVA vector as control.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide AQP identification; RNA-seq expression profiling under salt stress; qRT-PCR validation; VIGS using CLCrV-based constructs; salt stress assays by 400 mM NaCl irrigation and leaf-disk incubation; measurements of chlorophyll content, MDA, SOD, POD; statistical analysis with Student’s t-test.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS) of GhPIP2;7 in G. hirsutum followed by salt stress (400 mM NaCl) and physiological/biochemical measurements (chlorophyll, MDA, SOD, POD).
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate AQP genes were identified genome-wide using HMMER and transcriptome data; GhPIP2;7 was selected based on salt-induced expression from RNA-seq and qRT-PCR, then functionally analyzed via VIGS.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhPIP2;7 is a promising gene for improving salt tolerance in upland cotton; manipulation or selection of favorable GhPIP2;7 alleles/transcripts could enhance plant survival and maintain photosynthetic capacity under high salinity, providing a genetic resource for breeding salt-tolerant cotton varieties.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GhTIP2;1 also showed a modest role in salt stress response in Arabidopsis (enhanced expression of stress-related genes under NaCl with limited phenotypic improvement), indicating its primary contribution is to osmotic/drought tolerance rather than strong salt tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Public RNA-seq data for G. hirsutum TM-1 under PEG and NaCl used to profile GhTIP2;1 expression; qRT-PCR confirmed early induction after drought (15% PEG6000).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GhTIP2;1 positively regulates drought/osmotic stress tolerance: silencing in cotton increases sensitivity to PEG-induced osmotic stress with reduced root and leaf relative water content, whereas overexpression in Arabidopsis enhances drought (PEG) tolerance with better root growth and lower oxidative damage under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Overexpression of GhTIP2;1 reduces accumulation of H2O2 and MDA and increases proline content under salt and osmotic stress, suggesting improved ROS detoxification and osmolyte accumulation; associated with higher expression of AtP5CS (proline biosynthesis), AtNHX (Na+/H+ antiporter), and AtLEA (late embryogenesis abundant protein) under stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in osmotic/drought stress response and associated oxidative stress and ion homeostasis pathways; GhTIP2;1 overexpression enhances osmotic tolerance via increased proline accumulation and improved Na+ efflux, accompanied by induction of stress-responsive genes (AtP5CS, AtNHX, AtLEA).
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: GhTIP2;1 overexpression in Arabidopsis is associated with increased transcription of stress-related genes under osmotic stress: GhTIP2;1 -->(activation)--> AtP5CS; GhTIP2;1 -->(activation)--> AtNHX; GhTIP2;1 -->(activation)--> AtLEA (no significant differences under control conditions, but significantly higher expression in OE lines than WT after stress).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Localized predominantly to the vacuolar (tonoplast) membrane, as shown by 35S:GFP-GhTIP2;1 transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and GFP-tagged GhTIP2;1 in transgenic Arabidopsis roots.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In cotton, GhTIP2;1 is rapidly induced at early stages after drought/osmotic stress, with transcript levels peaking around 2 h after PEG treatment. In Arabidopsis ProGhTIP2;1:GUS lines, promoter activity is strong in cotyledons, rosette leaves, and roots under control conditions, but GUS staining decreases in leaves after 10–20% PEG6000 or 200 mM NaCl, while expression remains high in roots.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress induced by 13–15% PEG6000 in cotton and Arabidopsis; salt stress induced by 150–200 mM NaCl in expression and OE assays.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (osmotic/drought and salt stress) mediated by a tonoplast aquaporin in upland cotton
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress – drought/osmotic stress tolerance (with additional effects on salt stress responses)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 13% PEG6000, GhTIP2;1-silenced cotton plants show significantly reduced leaf and root relative water content and altered chlorophyll content compared with TRV:GFP controls. In GhTIP2;1-overexpressing Arabidopsis, root elongation is less inhibited by 15% PEG6000 than in WT; under PEG and 150 mM NaCl, transgenic plants accumulate significantly less MDA and H2O2 and significantly more proline than WT (exact values shown in Figures 5 and 6).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Silencing GhTIP2;1 in cotton (TRV:GhTIP2;1) causes wilting and yellowing of entire plants under PEG-induced drought; GhTIP2;1-overexpressing Arabidopsis shows better growth and greener phenotype than WT under PEG stress, with modest or no visible improvement under NaCl compared with drought.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: G. hirsutum cultivars GX100-2 and Zhong79 for qRT-PCR and PEG drought assays; TRV:GhTIP2;1 and TRV:GFP constructs for VIGS in cotton; Arabidopsis thaliana Col-0 as wild type and GhTIP2;1-overexpressing and ProGhTIP2;1:GUS transgenic lines for functional and promoter activity analyses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide AQP and expression analysis; qRT-PCR under PEG and NaCl stresses; VIGS in cotton using TRV vectors; drought/osmotic assays with 13% PEG6000 in cotton; Arabidopsis transformation by floral dip; selection of OE lines; stress assays on MS medium with 15% PEG6000 and 150 mM NaCl; root length measurements; ProGhTIP2;1:GUS histochemical staining under PEG and NaCl; GFP-based subcellular localization in Arabidopsis roots and Nicotiana leaves; assays of proline, MDA, H2O2; qRT-PCR of AtP5CS, AtNHX, and AtLEA; statistical analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: (1) Virus-induced gene silencing (TRV:GhTIP2;1) in cotton followed by 13% PEG6000 treatment and measurement of chlorophyll and relative water content. (2) Stable overexpression of GhTIP2;1-eGFP in Arabidopsis (Col-0) with stress assays under 15% PEG6000 and 150 mM NaCl, including root growth, proline, MDA, H2O2, and stress-related gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: GhTIP2;1 was identified from the genome-wide GhAQP set and RNA-seq/qRT-PCR expression analysis as a drought/PEG-inducible TIP gene, then cloned into TRV for VIGS in cotton and into pCAMBIA1300-eGFP for overexpression and promoter:GUS analysis in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhTIP2;1 is a strong candidate gene for improving drought and osmotic stress tolerance in cotton. Its positive effect on water status, ROS attenuation, and proline accumulation suggests that manipulating GhTIP2;1 (via transgenic approaches or future allele-based selection) could enhance cotton performance in water-deficit and saline environments, providing a valuable resource for breeding stress-resilient cultivars.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Both GhTIP1;1 and GhTIP2;1 silencing reduce drought tolerance, but GhTIP2;1 was chosen for further overexpression and mechanistic studies, suggesting GhTIP1;1 has a supportive but less extensively characterized role.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression upregulation after drought (15% PEG6000) identified from RNA-seq and confirmed by qRT-PCR.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhTIP1;1 in cotton reduces drought/osmotic tolerance, leading to wilting and yellowing of whole plants and reduced root and leaf relative water content under PEG-induced stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Participates in drought/osmotic stress response pathway, likely contributing to maintenance of cellular water status via tonoplast water transport.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Localized to the vacuolar membrane, based on 35S:GFP-GhTIP1;1 transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: GhTIP1;1 is upregulated at early stages after drought/PEG treatment, with expression peaking around 2 h after osmotic stress.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress induced by 13% PEG6000 in hydroponic culture for VIGS plants.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of a tonoplast aquaporin in drought/osmotic stress tolerance in upland cotton
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under 13% PEG6000, TRV:GhTIP1;1 plants show significantly reduced leaf and root relative water content and altered chlorophyll content compared with TRV:GFP controls (quantitative values provided in Figure 5).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: TRV:GhTIP1;1 cotton plants display pronounced wilting and yellowing under osmotic stress, while mock TRV:GFP plants remain relatively turgid and green.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: G. hirsutum cultivars GX100-2 and Zhong79; TRV:GhTIP1;1 and TRV:GFP constructs used for VIGS in cotton seedlings grown in Hoagland solution.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR to assess GhTIP1;1 expression under PEG stress; VIGS using TRV-based vectors; 13% PEG6000 drought treatment; phenotyping for wilting and yellowing; measurement of chlorophyll content and root/leaf relative water content; statistical analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing (TRV:GhTIP1;1) in cotton followed by 13% PEG6000 treatment and measurement of chlorophyll content and root and leaf relative water content.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from GhAQP family and drought-responsive expression profiling as a PEG-inducible TIP; cloned into TRV vector for VIGS-based functional analysis in cotton.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhTIP1;1 represents an additional candidate gene for enhancing drought/osmotic stress tolerance in cotton; modulating its expression could improve maintenance of tissue water status under water-deficit conditions.
